268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                    parent.addAfter(createComma(), this)
326→                                }
327→                            }
328→                        } else {
329→                            val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                            prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                        }
332→                    }
333→                }
334→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
335→                emit(
336→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
337→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
338→                    true,
339→                )
340→                if (autoCorrect) {
341→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
342→                }
343→            }
344→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
345→        }
346→    }
347→
348→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
349→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
350→        parents()
351→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
352→            .lineIndent()
353→
354→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
355→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
356→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
358→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
359→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
360→            // special handling for collection literal
361→            // @Annotation([
362→            //    "something",
363→            // ])
364→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
365→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
366→        }
367→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
368→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
369→    }
370→
371→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
372→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
373→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
374→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
375→        } else {
376→            false
377→        }
378→
379→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
380→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
381→            this
382→        } else {
383→            prevCodeLeaf()
384→        }
385→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
386→    }
387→
388→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
389→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
390→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
391→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
392→                return true
393→            }
394→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
395→        }
396→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
397→    }
398→
399→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
400→        /**
401→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
402→         */
403→        EXISTS,
404→
405→        /**
406→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
407→         */
408→        MISSING,
409→
410→        /**
411→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
412→         */
413→        NOT_EXISTS,
414→
415→        /**
416→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
417→         */
418→        REDUNDANT,
419→    }
420→
421→    public companion object {
422→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
423→
424→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
425→            EditorConfigProperty(
426→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
427→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
428→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
429→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
430→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
431→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
432→                        "trailing comma when set.",
433→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
434→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
435→                ),
436→                defaultValue = true,
437→                defaultAndroidValue = false,
438→            )
439→
440→        @Deprecated(
441→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
442→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
443→        )
444→        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
445→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
446→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
447→
448→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
449→            CLASS,
450→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
451→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
452→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
453→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
454→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
455→            WHEN_ENTRY,
456→        )
457→    }
458→}
459→

OK, I notice my earlier write already changed the comma insertion (lines 325 and 328-331). Let me now also fix the `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition` method. But first, let me reconsider whether the comma fix I already made is correct for the textual output.

With my current changes (comma added to CLASS_BODY via `parent.addAfter(createComma(), this)` where `this` is the enum entry):

After the transformation, the CLASS_BODY tree would be:
```
ENUM_ENTRY(B \n    )   ← includes B identifier + whitespace
COMMA(,)               ← added by parent.addAfter(createComma(), this)
SEMICOLON(;)           ← added by parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
```

The text would be: `B\n    ,;` which is WRONG. We need `B,\n    ;`.

So my comma fix is incorrect for textual output. Let me revert it and instead keep the comma inside the ENUM_ENTRY (as in the original code), but fix `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition` to also look for semicolons in CLASS_BODY.

Additionally, I need to fix the NoSemicolonsRule to handle semicolons that are direct children of CLASS_BODY.

Let me write the complete fixed file:

content.text:
Now I need to understand the `isNoSemicolonRequiredAfter` function. After the trailing comma rule moves the semicolon from ENUM_ENTRY to CLASS_BODY:

1. `node.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY` is `false` (it's now CLASS_BODY)
2. `prevCodeLeaf` of the semicolon would be the comma (which was inserted inside the ENUM_ENTRY)
3. `prevCodeLeaf.treeParent` would be the ENUM_ENTRY
4. `parent = prevCodeLeaf.treeParent?.psi` = the ENUM_ENTRY's PSI
5. It's not a KtLoopExpression or KtIfExpression
6. Falls through to `return true` (no semicolon required) → incorrectly reports unnecessary semicolon

The fix needs to handle the case where the semicolon is a direct child of CLASS_BODY and the previous code leaf is inside an ENUM_ENTRY (the comma that was just added). In this case, the semicolon IS required (to separate enum entries from class members).

content.text:
All tests pass. Now let me think more carefully about my changes and verify they address the issue.

The issue has two violations:
1. `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` reports "Missing trailing comma before `}`" on second lint pass
2. `no-semi` reports "Unnecessary semicolon" on second lint pass

Let me trace through my fixes for both:

**Fix 1: `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition`**

After the first pass (with autoCorrect), the semicolon has been moved from inside ENUM_ENTRY to CLASS_BODY. On the second pass (lint only), `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition` is called again:
1. Looks for semicolon inside last ENUM_ENTRY → not found (it was moved)
2. **NEW**: Looks for semicolon as direct child of CLASS_BODY → found!
3. Returns the semicolon

Now `reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore` is called with `inspectNode = semicolon` (in CLASS_BODY):
- `prevLeaf()` of the semicolon would be whitespace before the semicolon
- `findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()` → `prevCodeLeaf()` of the whitespace → should find the comma (inside the ENUM_ENTRY)
- Since the comma exists, `trailingCommaState = EXISTS` (because it's multiline)
- In the EXISTS case, since the treeParent is CLASS_BODY (not WHEN_ENTRY), nothing happens → no violation reported

This fixes the `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` violation.

**Fix 2: `NoSemicolonsRule.isNoSemicolonRequiredAfter`**

After the first pass, the semicolon is in CLASS_BODY. The `NoSemicolonsRule` runs on the second pass:
1. `node.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY` → false (it's CLASS_BODY)
2. **NEW**: `node.treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY && prevCodeLeaf.treeParent?.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY` → true!
   - `prevCodeLeaf` is the comma, whose treeParent is the ENUM_ENTRY
3. `node.isLastCodeLeafBeforeClosingOfClassBody()` → checks if the semicolon is the last code leaf before `}`
   - If there ARE class members (like `companion object`), it's NOT the last code leaf → returns false
   - So `isNoSemicolonRequiredAfter` returns false → the semicolon IS required → no violation reported

Wait, but `isNoSemicolonRequiredAfter` returning `false` means `isNoSemicolonRequiredAfter(node)` is `false`, so the `if` condition `nextLeaf.doesNotRequirePreSemi() && isNoSemicolonRequiredAfter(node)` is `false`, so no "Unnecessary semicolon" is emitted. 

But wait, what about when there are NO class members after the semicolon? In that case, `isLastCodeLeafBeforeClosingOfClassBody()` returns `true`, and `isNoSemicolonRequiredAfter` returns `true`, which means the semicolon IS unnecessary. That's correct behavior — if there are no class members after the enum entries, the semicolon is indeed unnecessary and should be removed.

This all looks correct. Let me now also consider edge cases:

1. **Enum with no semicolon and no class members (just entries):**
   - `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition` → no semicolon inside ENUM_ENTRY, no semicolon in CLASS_BODY → returns `lastChildNode` (`}`)
   - This is correct behavior (comma before `}`)

2. **Enum already correctly formatted (with trailing comma and semicolon in CLASS_BODY):**
   - This would be the case when the code was correctly parsed from:
     ```kotlin
     enum class Shape {
         SQUARE,
         TRIANGLE,
         ;
     }
     ```
   - In this case, `;` is a direct child of CLASS_BODY (from parsing)
   - `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition` → no semicolon inside ENUM_ENTRY → **NEW**: finds semicolon in CLASS_BODY → returns it
   - `reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore` with semicolon in CLASS_BODY:
     - `prevLeaf()` = whitespace before `;`
     - `prevCodeLeaf` = the `,` (comma in CLASS_BODY)
     - `findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull` → finds the comma → trailingCommaState = EXISTS
     - No violation → correct!

Wait, but what about the case where there's already a semicolon in CLASS_BODY AND the trailing comma is already NOT allowed? Let me trace:

For code:
```kotlin
enum class Shape {
    SQUARE,
    TRIANGLE,
    ;
    fun print() = name()
}
```

With `allowTrailingComma = false`:

content.text:
I see. The test framework's `isFormattedAs` method calls `format()`, which formats the code (applies all rules with autocorrect). It checks that the formatted output matches the expected code. The `hasLintViolation` checks lint violations separately.

The key thing is: the `hasLintViolation` check runs the rules in lint-only mode (no autocorrect). The `isFormattedAs` runs in format mode (with autocorrect). These are separate operations.

In the lint-only mode for the trailing comma rule test, it only checks violations from the specific rule being tested, not from other rules. So the NoSemicolonsRule's violations don't show up in the trailing comma rule's test.

In the format mode, the test framework calls the `format` method from KtLint, which runs the two-pass approach. But the assertion only checks the TEXT output, not the intermediate violations. So even if there are violations in the second pass (like the ones from the issue), the test would still pass as long as the textual output is correct.

This explains why the existing tests pass even without my fix - they don't check for the false violations from the second pass.

Now, the issue is specifically about the CLI tool's behavior when running `ktlint --format`, which reports violations AND formats the code. The violations from the second pass are what the user sees.

Let me also think about whether there could be issues with my `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition` change for other cases.

Consider a case where there's a semicolon in CLASS_BODY that is NOT related to enum entries. Actually, in an enum class, the semicolon in CLASS_BODY is always the one that separates enum entries from class members. There shouldn't be other semicolons that are direct children of CLASS_BODY (semicolons in functions would be inside those function nodes).

Wait, what if there's code like:
```kotlin
enum class Shape {
    SQUARE,
    TRIANGLE
    ;
    val x = 1;
}
```

The `val x = 1;` — the `;` would be inside the PROPERTY node, not a direct child of CLASS_BODY. And `children().firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }` — `children()` only returns direct children. So this would only find the standalone `;` between enum entries and class members, not semicolons inside class member declarations. That's correct.

Let me also consider whether using `firstOrNull` vs `lastOrNull` matters. Since there should be at most one standalone semicolon in the CLASS_BODY (the one separating enum entries from class members), `firstOrNull` is fine.

Now let me verify my changes compile and all existing tests pass:

content.text:
Good. Both changes look correct. Let me summarize the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
When formatting an enum class with member definitions and missing trailing commas, the `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule` correctly formats the code but creates an inconsistent AST. When the second lint pass runs on the modified AST, two false violations are reported:

1. **`trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`**: After the semicolon is moved from inside the ENUM_ENTRY to the CLASS_BODY, `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()` can no longer find it (it only looks inside the last ENUM_ENTRY), so it falls back to `lastChildNode` (the `}` brace) and reports a missing trailing comma before `}`.

2. **`no-semi`**: After the semicolon is moved from the ENUM_ENTRY to the CLASS_BODY, `isNoSemicolonRequiredAfter()` doesn't recognize it as an enum-related semicolon (it only checks for `node.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY`), and incorrectly reports it as unnecessary.

### Fix

**File 1: `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`** - Modified `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()`:
- Added a fallback to also look for a semicolon that is a direct child of CLASS_BODY (in addition to looking inside the last ENUM_ENTRY)
- This handles the case where the trailing comma rule has already been applied and the semicolon was moved from the ENUM_ENTRY to the CLASS_BODY

**File 2: `NoSemicolonsRule.kt`** - Modified `isNoSemicolonRequiredAfter()`:
- Added a check for when the semicolon is a direct child of CLASS_BODY and the previous code leaf is inside an ENUM_ENTRY
- This handles the case where the trailing comma rule has moved the semicolon from the ENUM_ENTRY to the CLASS_BODY
- Uses the same `isLastCodeLeafBeforeClosingOfClassBody()` logic to determine if the semicolon is required

 75→

200→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)

216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()

229→    }

231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =

235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                        }
328→
329→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359→            // special handling for collection literal
360→            // @Annotation([
361→            //    "something",
362→            // ])
363→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365→        }
366→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374→        } else {
375→            false
376→        }
377→
378→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380→            this
381→        } else {
382→            prevCodeLeaf()
383→        }
384→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391→                return true
392→            }
393→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394→        }
395→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396→    }
397→
398→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
399→        /**
400→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
401→         */
402→        EXISTS,
403→
404→        /**
405→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
406→         */
407→        MISSING,
408→
409→        /**
410→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
411→         */
412→        NOT_EXISTS,
413→
414→        /**
415→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
416→         */
417→        REDUNDANT,
418→    }
419→
420→    public companion object {
421→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
422→
423→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
424→            EditorConfigProperty(
425→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
426→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
427→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
428→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
429→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
430→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
431→                        "trailing comma when set.",
432→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
433→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
434→                ),
435→                defaultValue = true,
436→                defaultAndroidValue = false,
437→            )
438→
439→        @Deprecated(
440→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
441→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
442→        )
443→        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
444→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
445→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
446→
447→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
448→            CLASS,
449→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
450→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
451→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
452→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
453→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
454→            WHEN_ENTRY,
455→        )
456→    }
457→}

619→    @Test
620→    fun `Given that a trailing comma is required on declaration site and unused imports do not affect each other`() {
621→        val code =
622→            """
623→            package com.pinterest.ktlint
624→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.Enum
625→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.EnumThree
626→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.EnumTwo
627→            data class TrailingCommaTest(
628→                val foo: String,
629→                val bar: Enum,
630→                val bar2: EnumTwo,
631→                val bar3: EnumThree
632→            )
633→            """.trimIndent()
634→        val formattedCode =
635→            """
636→            package com.pinterest.ktlint
637→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.Enum
638→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.EnumThree
639→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.EnumTwo
640→            data class TrailingCommaTest(
641→                val foo: String,
642→                val bar: Enum,
643→                val bar2: EnumTwo,
644→                val bar3: EnumThree,
645→            )
646→            """.trimIndent()
647→        trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat(code)
648→            // When running format mode, the rules are first executed in parallel to find linting errors. In this
649→            // process, no unused import are found because the trailing comma is not yet added to variable "bar3". Then
650→            // in the next stage the rules are run consecutively. Now the trailing comma rule is adding a trailing comma
651→            // after the type of variable "bar3". When the no-unused-import rule runs after the trailing-comma rule, it
652→            // was incorrectly seen as part of the type of variable "bar3" and a reference "EnumThree," (with the
653→            // trailing comma was added) which in turn resulted in not recognizing that the import of EnumThree actually
654→            // was used.
655→            .addAdditionalRuleProvider { NoUnusedImportsRule() }
656→            .withEditorConfigOverride(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY to true)
657→            .hasLintViolation(9, 24, "Missing trailing comma before \")\"")
658→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
659→    }

 48→/**
 49→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 50→ *
 51→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 52→ */
 53→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 54→    Rule(
 55→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58→                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 60→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 61→            ),
 62→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 63→        ),
 64→    ),
 65→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 66→
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() =
 74→        elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 77→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87→        when (node.elementType) {
 88→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→            else -> Unit
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
102→    ) {
103→        val inspectNode = node
104→            .children()
105→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
106→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
107→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
108→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
109→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
110→            emit = emit,
111→        )
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
115→        node: ASTNode,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
118→    ) {
119→        val inspectNode = node
120→            .children()
121→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
122→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123→            return
124→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
125→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
126→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
127→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
128→            emit = emit,
129→        )
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun visitValueList(
133→        node: ASTNode,
134→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
135→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136→    ) {
137→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
138→            node
139→                .children()
140→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
141→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
142→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
143→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
144→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
145→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
146→                        emit = emit,
147→                    )
148→                }
149→        }
150→    }
151→
152→    private fun visitTypeList(
153→        node: ASTNode,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156→    ) {
157→        val inspectNode = node
158→            .children()
159→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
160→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
161→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
162→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
163→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
164→            emit = emit,
165→        )
166→    }
167→
168→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
169→        node: ASTNode,
170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
172→    ) {
173→        val psi = node.psi
174→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
175→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
176→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
177→            return
178→        }
179→
180→        val inspectNode = node
181→            .children()
182→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
183→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
184→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
185→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
186→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
187→            emit = emit,
188→        )
189→    }
190→    private fun visitClass(
191→        node: ASTNode,
192→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
194→    ) {
195→        val psi = node.psi
196→        require(psi is KtClass)
197→
198→        node
199→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
200→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
201→            ?.takeUnless {
202→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204→            }?.let { classBody ->
205→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210→                    emit = emit,
211→                )
212→            }
213→    }
214→
215→    /**
216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()
226→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227→
228→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229→    }
230→
231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233→            children()
234→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                        }
328→
329→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359→            // special handling for collection literal
360→            // @Annotation([
361→            //    "something",
362→            // ])
363→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365→        }
366→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374→        } else {
375→            false
376→        }
377→
378→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380→            this
381→        } else {
382→            prevCodeLeaf()
383→        }
384→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391→                return true
392→            }
393→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394→        }
395→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396→    }
397→
398→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
399→        /**
400→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
401→         */
402→        EXISTS,
403→
404→        /**
405→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
406→         */
407→        MISSING,
408→
409→        /**
410→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
411→         */
412→        NOT_EXISTS,
413→
414→        /**
415→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
416→         */
417→        REDUNDANT,
418→    }
419→
420→    public companion object {
421→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
422→
423→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
424→            EditorConfigProperty(
425→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
426→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
427→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
428→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
429→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
430→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
431→                        "trailing comma when set.",
432→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
433→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
434→                ),
435→                defaultValue = true,
436→                defaultAndroidValue = false,
437→            )
438→
439→        @Deprecated(
440→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
441→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
442→        )
443→        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
444→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
445→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
446→
447→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
448→            CLASS,
449→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
450→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
451→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
452→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
453→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
454→            WHEN_ENTRY,
455→        )
456→    }
457→}

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
  6→
  7→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
  8→
  9→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 10→
 11→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
 12→
 13→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
 14→
 15→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
 16→```editorconfig
 17→[*.{kt,kts}]
 18→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
 19→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
 20→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
 21→```
 22→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
 23→
 24→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
 25→
 26→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
 27→```
 28→KtLintRuleEngine(
 29→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
 30→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
 31→        EditorConfig
 32→            .builder()
 33→            .section(
 34→                Section
 35→                    .builder()
 36→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
 37→                    .properties(
 38→                        Property
 39→                            .builder()
 40→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
 41→                            .value("enabled"),
 42→                    ),
 43→            )
 44→            .build()
 45→    )
 46→)
 47→```
 48→If the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
 49→
 50→If you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).
 51→
 52→### Added
 53→
 54→### Removed
 55→
 56→### Fixed
 57→* Fix with array-syntax annotations on the same line as other annotations `annotation` ([#1765](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1765))
 58→* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 59→* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 60→* Allow diacritics in names of classes, functions packages, and properties `class-naming`, `function-naming`, `package-name`, `property-naming` ([#1757](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1757))
 61→* Prevent violation of `file-name` rule on code snippets ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 62→* Clarify that API Consumers have to enable experimental rules ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 63→* Trim spaces in the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`'s entries ([#1770](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1770))
 64→
 65→### Changed

 67→## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
 68→
 69→### Added
 70→
 71→### Removed
 72→
 73→### Fixed
 74→
 75→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 76→* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
 77→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
 78→* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 79→* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 80→* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
 81→
 82→### Changed
 83→
 84→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.
 85→
 86→## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 87→
 88→### Indent rule
 89→
 90→The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 91→
 92→### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 93→
 94→In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 95→
 96→The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 97→
 98→### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 99→
100→If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.

 48→/**
 49→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 50→ *
 51→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 52→ */
 53→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 54→    Rule(
 55→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58→                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 60→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 61→            ),
 62→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 63→        ),
 64→    ),
 65→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 66→
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() =
 74→        elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 77→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87→        when (node.elementType) {
 88→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→            else -> Unit
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
102→    ) {
103→        val inspectNode = node
104→            .children()
105→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
106→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
107→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
108→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
109→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
110→            emit = emit,
111→        )
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
115→        node: ASTNode,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
118→    ) {
119→        val inspectNode = node
120→            .children()
121→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
122→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123→            return
124→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
125→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
126→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
127→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
128→            emit = emit,
129→        )
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun visitValueList(
133→        node: ASTNode,
134→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
135→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136→    ) {
137→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
138→            node
139→                .children()
140→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
141→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
142→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
143→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
144→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
145→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
146→                        emit = emit,
147→                    )
148→                }
149→        }
150→    }
151→
152→    private fun visitTypeList(
153→        node: ASTNode,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156→    ) {
157→        val inspectNode = node
158→            .children()
159→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
160→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
161→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
162→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
163→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
164→            emit = emit,
165→        )
166→    }
167→
168→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
169→        node: ASTNode,
170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
172→    ) {
173→        val psi = node.psi
174→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
175→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
176→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
177→            return
178→        }
179→
180→        val inspectNode = node
181→            .children()
182→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
183→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
184→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
185→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
186→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
187→            emit = emit,
188→        )
189→    }
190→    private fun visitClass(
191→        node: ASTNode,
192→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
194→    ) {
195→        val psi = node.psi
196→        require(psi is KtClass)
197→
198→        node
199→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
200→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
201→            ?.takeUnless {
202→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204→            }?.let { classBody ->
205→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210→                    emit = emit,
211→                )
212→            }
213→    }
214→
215→    /**
216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()
226→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227→
228→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229→    }
230→
231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233→            children()
234→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                    parent.addAfter(createComma(), this)
326→                                }
327→                            }
328→                        } else {
329→                            val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                            prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                        }
332→                    }
333→                }
334→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
335→                emit(
336→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
337→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
338→                    true,
339→                )
340→                if (autoCorrect) {
341→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
342→                }
343→            }
344→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
345→        }
346→    }
347→
348→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
349→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
350→        parents()
351→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
352→            .lineIndent()
353→
354→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
355→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
356→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
358→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
359→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
360→            // special handling for collection literal
361→            // @Annotation([
362→            //    "something",
363→            // ])
364→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
365→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
366→        }
367→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
368→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
369→    }
370→
371→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
372→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
373→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
374→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
375→        } else {
376→            false
377→        }
378→
379→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
380→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
381→            this
382→        } else {
383→            prevCodeLeaf()
384→        }
385→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
386→    }
387→
388→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
389→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
390→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
391→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
392→                return true
393→            }
394→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
395→        }
396→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
397→    }
398→
399→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
400→        /**
401→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
402→         */
403→        EXISTS,
404→
405→        /**
406→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
407→         */
408→        MISSING,
409→
410→        /**
411→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
412→         */
413→        NOT_EXISTS,
414→
415→        /**
416→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
417→         */
418→        REDUNDANT,
419→    }
420→
421→    public companion object {
422→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
423→
424→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
425→            EditorConfigProperty(
426→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
427→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
428→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
429→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
430→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
431→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
432→                        "trailing comma when set.",
433→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
434→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
435→                ),
436→                defaultValue = true,
437→                defaultAndroidValue = false,
438→            )
439→
440→        @Deprecated(
441→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
442→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
443→        )
444→        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
445→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
446→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
447→
448→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
449→            CLASS,
450→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
451→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
452→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
453→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
454→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
455→            WHEN_ENTRY,
456→        )
457→    }
458→}
